steps.title: Calculate the cost per tablet including tax
steps.content:
Each tablet costs \$156. With 8% tax applied to the total order, the effective cost per tablet is:
$$156 \times 1.08 = 168.48$$

steps.title: Find the maximum number of tablets the budget allows
steps.content:
Set up the constraint: if the school orders $n$ tablets, the total cost is $168.48n$, and this must not exceed \$42,500:
$$168.48n \leq 42,500$$
$$n \leq \frac{42,500}{168.48} \approx 252.3$$
Since the school can only order whole tablets, the maximum number is 252.
